proposals.fieldLabel: Claude Opus 4.6 capability and context window stat
proposals.proposedValue: Claude Opus 4.6 — 1M token context window with 128K output capacity, enabling sustained multi-step reasoning and long-horizon autonomy
proposals.reason: The 5x context expansion and output capacity significantly changes the cost/capability tradeoff framework and selection criteria. This update reflects the current model capabilities as of March 2026 and directly impacts when to recommend Opus 4.6 over other models.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 1 — Update model family context windows and capability tradeoffs: Claude Opus 4.6 now has 1M token context (5x expansion) and 128K output capacity, requiring re-framing of cost/capability selection framework around sustained multi-step reasoning and long-horizon autonomy

proposals.fieldLabel: New lesson on adaptive thinking and context compaction
proposals.proposedValue: Claude 4.6 introduces adaptive thinking—a shift from fixed context budgets to dynamic reasoning depth. Instead of allocating a static amount of your context window to reasoning, Claude now adjusts thinking depth based on task complexity, allowing longer sessions with better resource management through context compaction strategies.
proposals.reason: Claude 4.6 introduces adaptive thinking and context compaction as core features that fundamentally change how context management works. This lesson addresses the shift from fixed budget allocation to dynamic reasoning depth, which is a significant evolution in the context management paradigm covered by Module 4.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 4 — Add adaptive thinking and context compaction strategies: Claude 4.6 introduces adaptive thinking (dynamic reasoning depth, not binary) and context compaction for longer sessions, shifting context management from fixed budget allocation to dynamic reasoning depth

proposals.fieldLabel: New lesson on Structured Outputs with Strict Mode
proposals.proposedValue: For production systems that require guaranteed schema compliance, strict mode uses constrained decoding to ensure Claude's output always matches your JSON schema exactly. Strict mode (available 2026+) is production-ready and eliminates the need for retry logic or validation wrappers around structured outputs.
proposals.reason: Action item requests adding a comprehensive section on strict mode (production-ready 2026) with subsections on strict mode tool use, schema design best practices, and decision framework. This is substantial new content not present in current Module 5.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 5 — Add structured outputs with strict mode section: Strict mode (production-ready 2026) guarantees schema-compliant JSON via constrained decoding; add subsection on strict mode tool use, schema design best practices, and when to use strict: true vs JSON mode

proposals.fieldLabel: New lesson: Claude Code 2026 feature updates
proposals.proposedValue: New lesson covering auto-plan mode, auto-memory learning, voice input (20 languages), /claude-api skill, MCP elicitation dialogs, /effort command, InstructionsLoaded hooks, ExitWorktree tool, and updated permission auto-approval allowlist
proposals.reason: Module 3 needs to reflect the 2026 Claude Code feature releases documented in the research. These are substantial, production-ready features that significantly impact the Claude Code experience and should be taught as part of the first session.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 3 — Update Claude Code tool inventory and features: Add auto-plan mode (now automatic on substantial prompts), auto-memory learning mechanism, voice input (20 languages), `/claude-api` skill, MCP elicitation dialogs, `/effort` command, InstructionsLoaded hooks, ExitWorktree tool, and updated permission auto-approval allowlist

proposals.fieldLabel: New subsection: Auto-Plan Mode and Structured Planning
proposals.proposedValue: Claude Code automatically enters plan mode on substantial prompts, shifting toward spec-driven development. When you submit a complex or multi-step request, Claude Code pauses to think through the approach before writing code. This structured planning phase ensures careful feature design and reduces rework by aligning on the solution architecture first.
proposals.reason: Claude Code's 2026 auto-plan feature represents a significant shift in how developers interact with AI-assisted coding. This subsection documents the feature and explains its alignment with spec-driven development practices, filling a gap in Module 3's current coverage of the agentic loop.
proposals.rawActionItem: [ADD] Module 3 — Add "Auto-Plan Mode and Structured Planning" subsection: Document how Claude Code automatically enters plan mode on substantial prompts and its shift toward spec-driven development

proposals.fieldLabel: New subsection: Auto-Memory and Learning from Errors
proposals.proposedValue: Claude Code now includes Auto-Memory, a feature that enables Claude to learn from its own mistakes over time, improving accuracy and reducing repeated errors across sessions. This persistent memory mechanism tracks what went wrong, why it failed, and how to avoid similar issues in future interactions within the same project.
proposals.reason: Recent Claude Code feature updates (March 2026) introduced Auto-Memory as a core capability that fundamentally changes how Claude Code improves over time. This subsection documents this persistent memory mechanism for users learning Claude Code for the first time, ensuring they understand this learning capability from the start.
proposals.rawActionItem: [ADD] Module 3 — Add "Auto-Memory and Learning from Errors" subsection: Document persistent memory mechanisms that improve accuracy and reduce repeated errors across sessions

proposals.fieldLabel: Voice-First Workflows with Claude Code subsection
proposals.proposedValue: Claude Code now supports voice-to-text input across 20+ languages, enabling hands-free coding and natural interaction. Voice Mode removes friction between ideas and prompts, making it ideal for accessibility, rapid prototyping, and developers who prefer speaking over typing. Simply enable Voice Mode in your Claude Code session and start speaking—Claude transcribes and processes your input in real time.
proposals.reason: Recent Claude Code updates (March 2026) introduced Voice Mode as a key feature that transforms developer workflows. Adding this subsection to Module 3 aligns the course with current product capabilities and highlights accessibility and productivity benefits that early learners should know about.
proposals.rawActionItem: [ADD] Module 3 — Add "Voice-First Workflows with Claude Code" subsection: Document voice-to-text input with 20-language support and accessibility benefits

proposals.fieldLabel: Claude Opus 4.6 capability detail in stat_reveal
proposals.proposedValue: 1M token context, deepest reasoning with adaptive thinking
proposals.reason: The research context indicates Opus 4.6 (beta, Feb 2026) now supports 1M token context. This should be reflected in the model family capabilities table to provide accurate current specifications.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 1 — Update model family capabilities table to reflect 1M token context for Opus 4.6 (beta, Feb 2026) and 200K for Sonnet 4.6, with accurate beta pricing

proposals.fieldLabel: Claude Opus 4.6 positioning in model family stat
proposals.proposedValue: Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6
proposals.reason: Sonnet 4.6 now achieves capability parity with Opus 4.5 on reasoning benchmarks (Feb 2026), warranting equal positioning in the model family introduction rather than treating it as a secondary option.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 1 — Revise Sonnet positioning from "balanced default" to emphasize Sonnet 4.6's capability parity with Opus 4.5 on reasoning benchmarks (Feb 2026)

proposals.fieldLabel: Module 3 subtitle framing
proposals.currentValue: From installation to your first multi-file edit
proposals.proposedValue: Multi-agent orchestration: Claude Code as a scalable agent platform
proposals.reason: The 2026 Claude Code v2.1.69 release introduces agent management features (agent_id, agent_type in hooks), /claude-api skill, and HTTP hooks for web service integration, positioning Claude Code as an orchestration platform rather than a pair-programming tool. The subtitle should reflect this architectural shift.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 3 — Reframe Claude Code introduction from "pair programming in terminal" to multi-agent orchestration platform reflecting 2026 scope

proposals.fieldLabel: New keyPoint on scheduled tasks (/loop command) in agentic loop lesson
proposals.proposedValue: Scheduled tasks with /loop: use /loop <interval> to run the agentic loop on a schedule (e.g., /loop 1h for hourly checks). Common use cases include error monitoring, automated testing, and periodic content updates. The loop respects the same permission model—you approve changes before they apply.
proposals.reason: Module 3 needs coverage of the /loop command introduced in Claude Code v2.1.69 (Mar 7, 2026 release). This belongs in the agentic loop lesson (l3_2) as a natural extension of the plan-act-observe-decide pattern.
proposals.rawActionItem: [ADD] Module 3 — Add section on scheduled tasks (`/loop` command) with scheduling intervals and use cases (error checks, tests) per Mar 7, 2026 release

proposals.fieldLabel: New subsection on Remote Control workflows for mobile/desktop supervision of Claude Code agents
proposals.proposedValue:
Remote Control Workflows

Clause Code agents can now be supervised and remotely controlled from mobile and desktop applications. Use the `/remote-control` command in Claude Code to spawn a supervisory session that accepts real-time directives from external devices. This enables mobile devices to monitor, interrupt, and redirect agent behavior without direct terminal access. Perfect for managing long-running agents across distributed systems or supervising agents deployed in production environments. Supervision sessions include live logs, permission checkpoints, and rollback capabilities.
proposals.reason: Claude Code v2.1.69 introduces enhanced hook events with agent_id and agent_type for better agent management and HTTP hooks for web service integration, enabling remote supervision workflows. This new capability should be documented in Module 3 as a core feature of Claude Code sessions.
proposals.rawActionItem: [ADD] Module 3 — Add subsection on Remote Control workflows for mobile/desktop supervision of Claude Code agents (Feb 24, 2026)

proposals.fieldLabel: Voice Mode subsection in lesson 3_1
proposals.proposedValue:
Voice Mode (Push-to-Talk)
Clause Code now supports Voice Mode across multiple surfaces. Push-to-talk is available in Claude Code on web, desktop, and mobile apps. Speech-to-text supports 20 languages as of Claude Code v2.1.69 (March 2026), with ongoing expansion. Voice Mode is currently rolling out globally and may have limited availability in some regions.
proposals.reason: Recent Claude Code v2.1.69 release added significant voice support improvements (20-language speech-to-text). This feature is directly relevant to Module 3's coverage of Claude Code setup and surfaces, and should be documented alongside the existing multi-surface installation information.
proposals.rawActionItem: [ADD] Module 3 — Add brief section on Voice Mode (push-to-talk) with language support and rollout status

proposals.fieldLabel: New lesson: /claude-api skill for API/SDK development
proposals.proposedValue: Claude Code v2.1.69 introduces the /claude-api skill, a built-in capability purpose-built for developers constructing applications on top of the Claude API and Anthropic SDK. Instead of switching between documentation tabs and your editor, you can invoke /claude-api to scaffold API calls, handle streaming responses, and manage tool use patterns directly within your Claude Code session.
proposals.reason: Module 3 teaches Claude Code fundamentals but does not yet cover the /claude-api skill introduced in v2.1.69 (Mar 4, 2026). This new lesson fills that gap and provides a worked example of a key developer feature for API/SDK work within Claude Code.
proposals.rawActionItem: [ADD] Module 3 — Add worked example covering `/claude-api` skill for API/SDK development within Claude Code (v2.1.69, Mar 4, 2026)

proposals.fieldLabel: New keyPoint on InstructionsLoaded hook for CLAUDE.md validation
proposals.proposedValue: InstructionsLoaded hook: automatically validate and run automation when CLAUDE.md changes. Use this to enforce project standards, sync configurations, or trigger CI/CD tasks without manual intervention.
proposals.reason: Claude Code v2.1.69 introduces the InstructionsLoaded hook as a major feature for automation and validation of instruction files. This directly extends the existing CLAUDE.md lesson (l3_3) with actionable guidance on the new lifecycle hook.
proposals.rawActionItem: [ADD] Module 3 or 6 — Add section on `InstructionsLoaded` hook for CLAUDE.md validation and automation (v2.1.69)

proposals.fieldLabel: New subsection: Prompt Caching Patterns for Production Workflows
proposals.proposedValue: Prompt caching allows you to cache large context blocks (system prompts, documents, code files) and reuse them across multiple requests, reducing latency and costs. This is critical for production agents that process high volumes of requests with stable, repetitive context.
proposals.reason: Module 4 currently covers context management strategies but does not address prompt caching, which became GA on Feb 4, 2026 and is essential for production agentic workflows. This addition aligns with the module's theme of context optimization and provides engineers with practical patterns for cost and latency reduction.
proposals.rawActionItem: [ADD] Module 4 — Add subsection on prompt caching patterns, breakpoint models, prefix-matching behavior, and cache invalidation for production agentic workflows (GA Feb 4, 2026)

proposals.fieldLabel: New lesson on structured outputs and strict mode
proposals.proposedValue: As of February 4, 2026, structured outputs are GA. For production systems, always enable `strict: true` mode to guarantee schema compliance. Claude will refuse to return data that violates your schema, eliminating parsing failures and hallucinations in structured fields.
proposals.reason: Structured outputs reached GA status on Feb 4, 2026, and `strict: true` mode is now a production best practice. The current Module 5 content does not cover structured outputs or schema validation, which are critical for building reliable agent systems.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 5 — Update structured outputs content to reflect GA status (Feb 4, 2026) and establish `strict: true` mode as production standard, with Pydantic/Zod schema validation examples

proposals.fieldLabel: Claude Opus 4.6 context window and capabilities
proposals.proposedValue: Claude Opus 4.6 — 1M token context window (GA March 2026), adaptive thinking with effort controls (low/medium/high/max), agent teams collaboration capability
proposals.reason: Research confirms Opus 4.6 now has 1M token context (not 200K), introduces adaptive thinking with granular effort controls, and enables multi-agent team collaboration. These are material capability updates that should be reflected in the model family overview.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 1 — Update model family specs: Opus 4.6 now has 1M token context (GA March 2026), not 200K; add Opus 4.6 adaptive thinking and effort controls (low/medium/high/max); add agent teams collaboration capability.

proposals.fieldLabel: Claude Opus 4.6 context window detail
proposals.proposedValue: Deepest reasoning with adaptive thinking. Supports 128K context window upper bound with 64K default.
proposals.reason: Opus 4.6 context window specifications have been updated. The research source confirms 128K upper bound with 64K default, replacing outdated 16K guidance across all context sizing examples.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 1 — Revise max_tokens guidance: Opus 4.6 supports 128K upper bound with 64K default, not 16K; update all context sizing examples accordingly.

proposals.fieldLabel: Claude Code installation and surfaces list
proposals.currentValue: Claude Code started in the terminal, but now it runs everywhere: your terminal, VS Code, JetBrains, a desktop app, the web, and even Slack.
proposals.proposedValue: Claude Code started in the terminal, but now it runs everywhere: your terminal, VS Code (native integration v2.1.70+), JetBrains, a desktop app, the web, and even Slack.
proposals.reason: Action item specifies VS Code native integration (v2.1.70+) should be added to Claude Code setup patterns. Research context confirms embedded VS Code extension is now available.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 3 — Add voice mode workflows (20 languages, v2.1.69+), /loop command for recurring tasks (v2.1.71), Auto Mode permission model, Computer Use integration (Pro/Max), and VS Code native integration (v2.1.70+) to Claude Code setup and usage patterns.

proposals.fieldLabel: New lesson on strict mode and constrained decoding for structured output
proposals.proposedValue: New lesson l5_4: 'Structured output with strict mode and constrained decoding.' Body: 'As of February 4, 2026, Claude supports strict: true mode in the API, enabling zero-parse-error guaranteed structured output through constrained decoding. This eliminates manual JSON validation and retry loops—Claude generates only valid JSON that matches your schema.' Key points: (1) 'strict: true mode (GA Feb 4, 2026): Append {"strict": true} to your tool schema or response format. Claude will only generate JSON that conforms to your schema.' (2) 'Zero parse errors: No more validation and retry loops. Every response is guaranteed valid—the model won\'t output malformed JSON.' (3) 'Constrained decoding: The API enforces your schema at generation time, not after. This is faster and more reliable than post-processing.' (4) 'Use case: Structured extraction (invoices, forms, logs), batch processing, multi-step workflows where invalid JSON would break the pipeline.' (5) 'Best practice: Define your schema in JSON Schema format. Let strict mode handle validation so you can focus on task logic.'
proposals.reason: The action item requests adding strict mode and constrained decoding content to Module 5's structured output section. This feature (GA Feb 4, 2026) is not currently covered in the existing lesson content and represents a significant capability that should replace manual validation patterns.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 5 — Replace manual JSON validation and retry-loop examples with strict: true mode section (GA Feb 4, 2026); add constrained decoding and zero-parse-error guarantees to structured output section.

proposals.fieldLabel: MCP protocol status and framing
proposals.currentValue: MCP is an open protocol created by Anthropic that standardizes how AI tools connect to external systems. Think of it as USB-C for AI: one standard plug that works with many devices.
proposals.proposedValue: MCP is an open protocol created by Anthropic that standardizes how AI tools connect to external systems. Think of it as USB-C for AI: one standard plug that works with many devices. As of v2.0.0-beta.3 (March 13, 2026), MCP is production-ready and includes OAuth 2.0 support, MCP elicitation protocol, and enterprise scalability features.
proposals.reason: The research context confirms MCP v2.0.0-beta.3 production readiness as of March 13, 2026, with OAuth 2.0 and enterprise features. The existing content uses experimental framing that should be updated to reflect production status.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 6 — Update MCP section to reflect v2.0.0-beta.3 (March 13, 2026) production readiness; add OAuth 2.0 support, MCP elicitation protocol, and enterprise scalability roadmap; remove experimental framing.

proposals.fieldLabel: New lesson on plugin persistent state capability
proposals.proposedValue: Claude Code v2.1.78 introduces plugin persistent state capability, allowing MCP servers and custom tools to maintain state across multiple sessions. This enables more sophisticated workflows where tools can remember previous interactions, cache expensive computations, and coordinate state across distributed agent systems.
proposals.reason: Research context confirms Claude Code v2.1.78 includes plugin persistent state as a new capability. This substantive feature warrants a new lesson in the Tools/Extensibility section to guide users on this advanced extensibility pattern.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 6 — Add plugin persistent state capability (v2.1.78) to Tools/Extensibility section.

proposals.fieldLabel: New lesson on AI-powered artifact generation for Pro+ users
proposals.proposedValue: { id: 'l2_5', heading: 'AI-powered artifact generation and persistent storage.', body: 'Pro+ users unlock advanced artifact capabilities: Claude can auto-generate artifacts based on conversation context, and artifacts are now stored persistently across sessions with version history. This means your designs, code, and documents are never lost and can be revisited, compared, and iterated on across multiple projects.', type: 'concept', keyPoints: [ { text: 'Persistent storage: all artifacts are saved automatically and accessible from any project or conversation.' }, { text: 'Version history: compare changes across artifact iterations and revert to earlier versions if needed.' }, { text: 'AI-powered generation: Claude proactively creates artifacts when it detects you need a deliverable, without waiting for explicit requests.' }, { text: 'Pro+ benefit: seamless workflow from idea to polished output with full audit trail.' } ] }
proposals.reason: Research context indicates Claude Code 2026 updates include enhanced artifact and storage features. This lesson captures the Pro+ value proposition for persistent artifact management.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 2 — Add AI-powered artifact generation and persistent storage features for Pro+ users; add /btw command (non-blocking questions) to co-work patterns section.

proposals.fieldLabel: New lesson on scheduled cloud tasks execution patterns
proposals.proposedValue: Claude v2.1.76+ enables scheduled cloud task execution patterns for workflow automation and agent scheduling. Integrate Claude with cloud schedulers (AWS EventBridge, Google Cloud Tasks, Azure Logic Apps) to trigger automated workflows on a schedule, enabling unattended agent execution, periodic data synchronization, and time-based automation pipelines.
proposals.reason: Claude Code v2.1.76 introduced advanced scheduling and checkpointing capabilities that enable production-grade workflow automation. This new lesson fills a gap in Module 7 by providing concrete patterns for scheduled execution and agent orchestration, directly supporting the module's focus on repeatable automation patterns.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 7 — Add scheduled cloud tasks execution patterns (v2.1.76+) for workflow automation and agent scheduling.

proposals.fieldLabel: New lesson on interactive artifacts and visualization generation
proposals.proposedValue: Claude Code now supports creating interactive artifacts and custom visualizations directly within Claude.ai. You can generate live-rendering React components, data visualizations, and interactive tools that update in real-time as you refine prompts. This transforms Claude from a text-based assistant into a rapid prototyping and visualization platform.
proposals.reason: Claude Code v2.1.76 (March 2026) introduces stable custom visualization and interactive artifact generation capabilities. This feature deserves explicit coverage in Module 2 alongside existing artifact types to reflect the expanded artifact ecosystem and empower users to leverage interactive prototyping.
proposals.rawActionItem: [ADD] Module 2 — Add section on interactive artifacts and visualization generation with Claude Code (stable feature, March 2026).
